quote_text: Nothing, Lucilius, is ours, except time.
reflection: What will you protect today because it is truly yours?

quote_text: Externals are not in my power: will is in my power.
reflection: What freedom is already available inside your own choice?

quote_text: No evil is great which is the last evil of all.
reflection: What fear shrinks when you remember even the last evil passes?

quote_text: All speak of themselves as having virtue and being good.
reflection: How can you look for the seed of goodness before judging someone?

quote_text: Take the things which relate to the body as far as the bare use.
reflection: What bodily comfort can stay useful without becoming the master?

quote_text: The universe is transformation: life is opinion.
reflection: What opinion is making a changing thing feel fixed?

quote_text: The true spirit can be tested, the spirit that will never consent to come under the jurisdiction of things external to ourselves.
reflection: What external pressure can test you without ruling you?

quote_text: You are a citizen of the world, and a part of it.
reflection: Which relationship tells you what justice requires today?

quote_text: The limiting of desires helps also to cure fears.
reflection: Which desire could you limit to quiet a needless fear?

quote_text: For the present is the only thing of which a man can be deprived.
reflection: What deserves attention because this moment is the only one in hand?

quote_text: As often as he falls, rises again with greater defiance than ever.
reflection: Where can you rise with more resolve than before?

quote_text: Welcome those whom you yourself can improve.
reflection: Who can you help without needing a crowd to notice?

quote_text: Be willing at length to be approved by yourself.
reflection: Where can self-approval matter more than being seen by others?

quote_text: Through not observing what is in the mind of another a man has seldom been seen to be unhappy.
reflection: Whose thoughts can you stop trying to manage today?

quote_text: You must persevere, must develop new strength by continuous study.
reflection: What steady practice will make courage less dramatic and more real?

quote_text: It is enough if every man fully discharges the work that is his own.
reflection: What work is yours to do for the common good?

quote_text: Pleasure, unless it has been kept within bounds, tends to rush headlong into the abyss of sorrow.
reflection: Which pleasure needs a boundary before it turns into regret?

quote_text: All that is from the gods is full of providence.
reflection: What would you see differently if you looked for order first?

quote_text: When we ought to share a friend's danger or that of our country, you must not consult the diviner whether you ought to share it.
reflection: How can freedom mean showing up for another person's danger?

quote_text: Do not trust her seeming calm; in a moment the sea is moved to its depths.
reflection: What preparation would help you stand steady if calm changes suddenly?

quote_text: Do not send your desire forward to it, but wait till it is opposite to you.
reflection: What are you reaching for before its proper time?

quote_text: To-day there is nothing wrong with it.
reflection: What future fear can you answer with today's actual facts?

quote_text: Women as well as men, he said, have received from the gods the gift of reason.
reflection: Where can shared reason guide you toward fairer treatment?

quote_text: Who then is the invincible? It is he whom none of the things disturb which are independent of the will.
reflection: What limitation can you meet without surrendering your will?

quote_text: The present alone can make no man wretched.
reflection: What past or future trouble can you set down for this present moment?

quote_text: Life is a warfare and a stranger's sojourn, and after fame is oblivion.
reflection: What ambition looks smaller when fame is measured against oblivion?

quote_text: The process is mutual; for men learn while they teach.
reflection: What can you teach today in a way that also teaches you?

quote_text: Reflect that a highwayman or an enemy may cut your throat.
reflection: What fragile assumption can make you braver if you face it plainly?

quote_text: If you have received the impression of any pleasure, guard yourself against being carried away by it.
reflection: What tempting impression should wait until your judgment catches up?

quote_text: Examine yourself; scrutinize and observe yourself in divers ways.
reflection: What honest self-examination would make tomorrow cleaner?
